detail.boundary: Current Electron dev-app N2 JANGTQ2 strict long-delta Responses loopback proof. The specific required tool_choice is suppressed for local non-Gemma loopback sessions and replaced with tool_choice=auto, preserving tool pressure without triggering the required-tool error path. This clears the strict N2 JANGTQ2 loopback tool-loop red follow-up only; it does not clear public tunnel parity, media/audio, N2 JANG_1L, packaging, signing, notarization, or full release readiness.

no_claims: No N2 JANG_1L work was performed.
No release, signing, notarization, PyPI, updater, download, or website action was performed.
This clears the strict N2 JANGTQ2 loopback tool loop proof from this lane, but does not clear unrelated N2 audio, installed-app release, MiMo, Gemma, or global release rows.

source_fix.diagnostic_addition: Panel request summaries now include a redacted tool_choice field so future live proof logs can distinguish required specific tool_choice, auto, and omitted tool_choice.
source_fix.fix: When a local vMLX loopback session explicitly names one built-in tool and a specific tool_choice would be suppressed, send non-required tool_choice='auto' instead. This keeps tool use enabled without forcing the specific required-tool path that previously produced tool_calls_required errors.
source_fix.root_cause: The previous loopback safety fix suppressed a specific required tool_choice for non-Gemma local vMLX loopback sessions, but also dropped tool_choice entirely. N2 JANGTQ2 then avoided the second requested run_command in the strict long-delta UI proof. The required hard-error was gone, but tool pressure was too weak for the second turn.

source_checks: cd panel && npm test -- tests/request-builder.test.ts -t 'tool_choice|loopback remote' -> 7 passed
cd panel && npm test -- tests/tool-auto-continue.test.ts tests/tool-status-responsiveness.test.ts -> 18 passed
cd panel && npm run typecheck -> pass

checks.release_boundary: This proof captures direct/gateway raw SSE for N2 JANGTQ2 Responses tool + tool-result continuation. It does not prove installed app, public tunnel, media, or release readiness.
